title: Boat Rental in Cuba Havana: Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Navigating the turquoise waters off Havana on a budget is easier than you think with BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cuba Havana or list your own. Starting from around €150 for a half-day shared tour, departure points like Marina Hemingway offer affordable access to the Gulf of Mexico without breaking the bank.

Where to set off in Cuba Havana

Most budget charters start from Marina Hemingway, located west of the city centre. This full-service marina offers easy access to the open sea and is the hub for day trips. Marina Tarará, east of Havana, is a quieter alternative popular with smaller motorboats. For a classic experience, inquire at Club Náutico Internacional Hemingway, which has slips for visiting yachts. Nearby Caleta de Bellamar (a small inlet) is great for a short swim stop, while the Gulf of Mexico itself is your playground.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (e.g. QuickSilver Activ 605 Open), Ideal for groups of 4, 6, half-day trips to nearby coves from €220; check the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP charter page for specs.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 380), Best for a full-day sailing experience with up to 8 guests, from €400.
  3. Cabin cruiser (e.g. Azimut 46), Comfortable for 6, 8, ideal for fishing trips, from €550 half-day.
  4. Crewed yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 55), All-inclusive with skipper for up to 10 guests, from €900 full day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Havana?

Budget boat rentals in Havana start at approximately €50 per person for shared tours and rise to €200+ per hour for private luxury yachts. Here is a typical price breakdown in euros:

  • Shared tour (3 hours): €50, €80 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €150, €250
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €400, €700
  • Crewed yacht full-day (8 hours): €800, €1,500

For verified options within your budget, browse day charters in Cuba Havana and compare prices instantly.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cuba Havana?

No, you do not need a licence to rent a crewed boat, but for bareboat (self-drive) charters a valid ICC or equivalent is generally required. The operator will confirm exact requirements for your nationality. High-season runs from November to April, when the trade winds are steady; avoid the hurricane months (June, November). The local breeze known as the viento del Este (easterly wind) can pick up in the afternoon, so plan morning departures.

For a quiet anchorage away from the crowds, motor north-west to Jaimanitas, a fishing village just 15 minutes from Marina Hemingway where you can tie up to a local paladar for lunch.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €1,500 (EUR)
  • Peak months: November, April
  • Trip length: 3, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (cash preferred)

Questions about boat rental in Cuba Havana

Can I rent a boat in Havana without a captain?

Yes, bareboat rentals are available if you hold a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent; otherwise a skipper is mandatory.

What is the best time of year for a boat trip in Havana?

The best weather is from November to April, with calm seas and average temperatures of 25°C, avoiding the rainy season.

How far is it from Havana to the nearest island by boat?

Isla de la Juventud is approximately 100 km south, a challenging day trip; the nearer coral cays of the Archipiélago de los Canarreos are about 50 km away.

Can I rent a luxury yacht in Havana?

Yes, boats such as the Azimut 46 and Sunseeker 55 are available with full crews, starting from €800 per day.

Whether you are a seasoned sailor or a first-time renter, securing a verified boat is simple through the BoatHire24 marketplace. If you own a boat, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach a global audience of travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cuba Havana and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
